top_signals.title: Oil prices spike over 4% as Iran and Israel trade strikes, escalating regional tensions
top_signals.summary: Oil rises on Monday amid heightened tensions in the Middle East, raising concerns that the conflict may drag on.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: RBI Seen Joining Asia’s Rate-Hike Push as Inflation Risks Rise
top_signals.summary: The Reserve Bank of India will likely tighten monetary policy in coming months, bringing it in line with regional peers that have turned more hawkish in response to inflationary pressures stemming from the Middle East conflict, economists said.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Horizons Middle East & Africa 6/8/2026
top_signals.summary: Horizons Middle East & Africa is your daily spotlight on one of the world's fastest-growing regions. Live from Dubai, we bring you the latest global markets and analysis, plus news-making interviews, with a special focus on MEA. All that and more, as you head to the office in the Gulf, pause for lunch in Hong Kong, or start your day in London or Johannesburg.  (Source: Bloomberg)
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Christine Lagarde: Women and leadership: widening the pipeline
top_signals.why_it_matters: Central-bank repricing affecting yields, FX, liquidity and equities.

top_signals.title: India Bond Inflows Hit One-Year High After Rupee Support Steps
top_signals.summary: India’s index-eligible bonds recorded their largest inflow in almost a year after authorities announced measures aimed at boosting foreign purchases of the nation’s debt and supporting the rupee.
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.

top_signals.title: Tate & Lyle Accepts Bid From Ingredion in Loss for London
top_signals.summary: Ingredion Inc. agreed to buy Tate & Lyle Plc for £2.7 billion ($3.6 billion), in a move that marks the end of the UK company’s near-century on the London Stock Exchange.
